title: Citrusy Dense Korean Shrimp Stir fry
description: A pescatarian-friendly Korean dish that combines succulent shrimp, refried beans, and citrus notes in a dense, crowd-pleasing stir-fry. It's perfect for a quick and satisfying dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the shrimp and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until pink and cooked through.
Remove the shrimp from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil.
Add the gochujang, soy sauce, and orange marmalade, and stir to combine.
Add the refried beans and stir to combine with the sauce.
Add the cooked shrimp back into the skillet and stir to combine with the sauce and refried beans.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with green onions, toasted sesame seeds, and serve immediately.
